evals.prompt: Start this session with Agent Feeds set up correctly. I want you to make the ambient context available to the model in the right prompt slot if possible, but don't dump raw state files into the conversation.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks whether the bundled CLI works, installs/checks background polling, checks stream health, generates the stable brief, and explains that the brief should go in a system-level or persistent prompt slot when the host supports it. It does not read raw ~/.agentfeeds state files or include volatile full stream data in the prompt.
assertions: The agent runs or proposes `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py admin polling status --json` before assuming background refresh is installed.
The agent runs or proposes `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py streams health --json` to detect missing, stale, or failing streams.
The agent runs or proposes `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py brief` for compact session-start context.
The answer says the brief belongs in a system-level or persistent prompt/context slot when available.
The agent does not read raw files under `~/.agentfeeds/state/` as the normal session-start path.

evals.prompt: Before searching the web, check whether Agent Feeds already knows anything about Alice and the launch brief. Use local subscribed state if it is available.
evals.expected_output: The agent searches local Agent Feeds state for Alice and launch brief, reads matching non-stale stream data if found, and answers from that local state. It only falls back to web/source-specific tools if no relevant local state exists or the stream is stale and cannot refresh.
assertions: The agent uses `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py search <topic> --json` rather than only stream metadata lookup for content lookup.
If a match is found, the agent reads the matching subscription with `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py streams read <subscription-id> --limit 20 --json` before answering.
The answer avoids web search when non-stale local Agent Feeds state covers the question.
The answer distinguishes local Agent Feeds state from durable memory.
The agent does not hand-read or edit Agent Feeds state files directly.

evals.prompt: Subscribe my project notes file at evals/files/project-notes.md as Project notes, then tell me what you set up.
evals.expected_output: The agent searches templates, chooses the built-in `local/file` template instead of authoring a new template, subscribes with the file path and title, checks stream health or reads the stream once, and reports the concrete subscription created.
assertions: The agent checks built-in templates before creating a local template.
The agent uses the built-in `local/file` template for the Markdown file.
The subscription command includes `path=evals/files/project-notes.md` or an equivalent resolved path and `--title "Project notes"`.
The agent validates the result with `streams health --json`, `streams read`, or an equivalent Agent Feeds CLI check.
The agent does not scaffold a new template for this common local-file source.

evals.prompt: Can Agent Feeds subscribe to my private SQLite task database? If there isn't already a suitable source, draft the safest local approach but don't run any mutating commands.
evals.expected_output: The agent searches existing templates first. If no built-in fits SQLite directly, it explains that a local read-only command template is appropriate, asks for or proposes an operator-approved argv-only read command, scaffolds/tests a local template only after that decision, and avoids mutating commands or secrets in YAML.
assertions: The agent starts with `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py templates find <query>` instead of immediately writing a template.
The agent chooses local template authoring only after explaining that no built-in template fits the SQLite source.
The proposed command template uses an argv array and is explicitly read-only.
The agent avoids shell strings, mutating commands, cloud/account changes, and secrets in template YAML.
The agent validates and tests the template with `admin templates validate` and `admin templates test` before subscribing.

evals.prompt: Agent Feeds seems stale. Figure out whether the background fetcher is actually working and tell me the smallest safe next step.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks polling status and stream health, distinguishes scheduler problems from per-stream errors/missing/stale state, and recommends a targeted fix such as installing polling, refreshing one stream, or reconfiguring a failed subscription. It does not refresh all streams unless necessary or requested.
assertions: The agent checks `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py admin polling status --json`.
The agent checks `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py streams health --json`.
The response distinguishes polling installation problems from stream-level fetch errors.
The recommended next action is targeted and safe, such as polling install, one-stream refresh, or subscription reconfiguration.
The agent does not run `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py refresh --all` as the first/default fix unless the user asked for a full refresh.

evals.prompt: I want ambient context from OpenAI's blog RSS feed and also from my private local dashboard command. Decide what should be a built-in/template subscription versus what should be locally authored.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the reusable RSS built-in for OpenAI's blog/feed URL and treats the private dashboard command as local template authoring. It explains why common source families belong in the catalog/built-ins while private operator-specific commands stay local unless they become broadly reusable.
assertions: The agent selects a built-in or parameterized RSS template for the public OpenAI blog/feed source.
The agent treats the private dashboard command as local template authoring rather than a built-in catalog contribution by default.
The response explains the built-in/local split using reuse, source family, privacy, and auth/operator-specific concerns.
The agent proposes upstreaming only if the local template becomes broadly reusable and does not contain private details.
The agent keeps command-based local authoring read-only and operator-approved.

evals.prompt: I run a personal agent on my Mac. Help me make my Calendar, Reminders, and recent inbox mail available as ambient Agent Feeds context, but do not approve commands on my behalf.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads the macOS personal source setup reference, installs the macOS templates, explains that Calendar/Reminders/Mail templates are pending local_command sources, tells the user to run approve-command themselves for each source they want, subscribes only after approval, and warns about macOS permission prompts.
assertions: The agent uses or proposes `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py admin macos install-templates`.
The agent identifies `macos/calendar-today`, `macos/reminders-open`, and `macos/mail-inbox-recent`.
The agent tells the user to run `python3 scripts/agentfeeds.py admin templates approve-command <template-id>` themselves.
The agent does not approve local_command templates on behalf of the user.
The response mentions macOS Automation or app-data permissions may be required on first refresh.
